description: POST /v1/tasks/:id/commands — role: viewer/owner. Viewer-issued reverse control, relayed live to a connected source or persisted in CommandStore.pending_commands otherwise (v1-architecture.md §1.4, §3). Shows the optional Idempotency-Key/command_id and ttl_ms override.

request_with_ttl_override.description: Optional ttl_ms overrides the 60000 default (v1-architecture.md §6, openapi ttl_ms range 1..86400000).

error_response_400_invalid_action.note: action must be one of pause | resume | stop — v2's action set is unchanged in v1.

notes: Field mapping (v1-architecture.md §4.4): the server builds the WS command body with task_id from the PATH param (build-release-3.2) and issued_by_device_id from the AUTHENTICATED identity — never from the request body. The body carries only action, and optionally ttl_ms and command_id.

Idempotency (v1-architecture.md §6, R-command-idem): the optional Idempotency-Key header (or a body command_id) is mapped to the WS command_id, so a retried POST with the same key does not enqueue a second command. ttl_ms defaults to 60000 ms when omitted.

Effect is observed indirectly: the source acting on this command surfaces as a subsequent task.event (e.g. no further progress events after pause), never as a direct 'command succeeded' payload on this response — this mirrors proto/realtime/SPEC.md §8's 'no separate command result message type' rule.
